method_atom: a mechanism by which an isolated, deterministic system with strictly linear time evolution and a discrete spectrum relaxes to stationary macroscopic values that are universal across widely differing initial conditions and match the prediction of a coarse-grained statistical ensemble. The key structural feature is that relaxation is a property of the individual stationary modes rather than of the dynamics: each stationary mode within a narrow band of the conserved control quantity already encodes the same ensemble-average value of the macroscopic observables, so any superposition simply dephases toward that common value, making a single stationary mode sufficient to compute ensemble averages and removing the need for chaotic trajectories.
object_atom: an isolated many-body quantum system evolving unitarily out of equilibrium, whose many-body energy eigenstates within a microcanonical energy window and whose macroscopic thermodynamic observables relax to standard statistical-mechanical (thermal) values.
brief_reason: What carries across is the field-neutral relaxation-via-stationary-modes mechanism (individual spectral components each reproducing the ensemble average, so superpositions dephase to it); what is domain-specific is the isolated quantum many-body system, its energy eigenstates, and thermal equilibrium.
